payload:
Collaboratively design an opionionated DSL for the ast-search query.

Phase 1: Examine current ast-search implementation and work to define what an acceptable query looks like, and what its core features & syntax are.
Phase 2: Iteratively expand on that core syntax and featureset, starting from first principles and progressively increasing in complexity.
Phase 3: Integrate with current search runtime and enable passing queries as arguments, similar to how grep accepts regex.

When complete:
- I ... [truncated, 833 chars total]

step-1 wave ready: task-1774904608-31c5 (code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec) — write DSL.md spec with EBNF grammar, shorthands, examples, rationale. artifact: .agents/scratchpad/implementation/ast-search-dsl/

DSL.md written at repo root: EBNF grammar, 15 shorthands, 10 worked examples, design rationale. npm test passes (17/17). artifact: DSL.md, task: task-1774904608-31c5, key: code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec

DSL.md: 3 concrete bugs — (1) grammar contradicts &&>|| precedence claim (flat EBNF, no levels); (2) bare-ident vs bare-expr ambiguous for known shorthands like 'this' (grammar silent, semantics hand-waves); (3) 'template' shorthand maps to TaggedTemplateExpression not TemplateLiteral — misleading name. Minor: import() shorthand grammar overly permissive; whitespace-required around > undocumented. task: task-1774904608-31c5, key: code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec

DSL.md: fixed all 5 issues — (1) EBNF now encodes &&>|| precedence via two-level grammar (and-clause inside expr-clause); (2) bare-ident vs bare-expr disambiguated: known shorthands always resolve as bare-expr with explicit rule in semantics; (3) template→TemplateLiteral, tagged→TaggedTemplateExpression; (4) import() special-cased in shorthand production; (5) whitespace-required around > documented. task: task-1774904608-31c5, key: code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec, artifact: DSL.md

DSL.md: grammar/semantics contradiction — semantics says '&&' is default for space-separated predicates (implicit AND) but EBNF requires explicit '&&'; also bare-expr negation semantics unspecified. task: task-1774904608-31c5, key: code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec, artifact: DSL.md

DSL.md fixed: removed implicit-AND claim from semantics (now says && must be explicit, space-separated tokens are parse error); added bare-expr negation semantics section. task: task-1774904608-31c5, key: code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec, artifact: DSL.md

task: task-1774904608-31c5, key: code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec, artifact: DSL.md — critical: bare-expr with boolean operators (e.g. 'this && await' no scope) is syntactically valid per grammar but semantically unspecified; semantics section only defines bare-expr for single tokens; fix: add semantic paragraph clarifying that &&/|| in bare-expr mode evaluate against the entire file body, or restrict bare-expr grammar to single expr-pred

task: task-1774904608-31c5, key: code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec, artifact: DSL.md — fixed bare-expr boolean operator semantics: added paragraph clarifying &&/|| in bare-expr mode evaluate against entire file body; added worked example 8 with this && await / this || await

task: task-1774904608-31c5, key: code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-01:write-dsl-spec, artifact: DSL.md — two bugs: (1) disambiguation rule uses 'this' (JS reserved word, impossible as function name) as its example, masking that shorthands like call/fn/arrow/template conflict with real JS identifiers; fix with a realistic example; (2) unknown shorthand behavior unspecified (grammar accepts any [a-z]+ but spec doesn't say whether unrecognized shorthands error or silently return nothing)

DSL.md fourth-review fixes: (1) disambiguation rule now uses 'template' as example (not 'this'), listing real JS identifiers that collide with shorthands; (2) unknown shorthand behavior now specified — falls through to bare-ident, no error

DSL.md fifth review: REJECTED — (1) critical: unknown-shorthand spec has internal contradiction: 'not preceded by a scope' condition in one sentence implies scope-query expression position has different behavior, but that behavior is never specified — setup > foo (unknown lowercase) is undefined; (2) secondary: disambiguation workaround 'use a PascalCase scope-query' is misleading — scope position already uses identifier not shorthand, correct advice is write template > [expr]

DSL.md fifth review fixes: (1) split unknown-shorthand paragraph into explicit bare-query vs expression-position cases — expression position is now a parse error, no more contradiction; (2) replaced misleading 'PascalCase scope-query' workaround with correct 'template > [expression]' advice noting scope uses identifier production

DSL.md sixth review: REJECTED — line 282 presents `template > [expression]` in backticks as literal syntax but [expression] is not a valid expr-atom per grammar ([A-Z]... or shorthand only); user copying this verbatim gets a parse error. Fix: use a concrete example like `template > this`. Secondary: 'rename the identifier' fallback is a dead end in a read-only search context.
DSL.md sixth review fixes: replaced template > [expression] placeholder with concrete template > this example; replaced dead-end 'rename identifier' advice with acknowledged DSL limitation note
DSL.md seventh review: both sixth-review fixes confirmed correct; no new bugs found; spec is internally consistent
step-1 DSL spec complete (passed 7th critic review); ready for step-2 DSL parser implementation (src/query.ts)
step-2 DSL parser: task task-1774906257-12bc (code-assist:ast-search-dsl:step-02:parser-core) — implement src/query.ts + src/__tests__/query.test.ts covering all DSL.md grammar forms

## Reason
consecutive_failures

## Status
Too many consecutive failures.
